XGVec2Hermite

Performs a Hermite spline interpolation, using the specified 2D vectors.

XGVECTOR2* XGVec2Hermite(
  XGVECTOR2* pOut,
  CONST XGVECTOR2* pV1,
  CONST XGVECTOR2* pT1,
  CONST XGVECTOR2* pV2,
  CONST XGVECTOR2* pT2,
  FLOAT s
);

Parameters

pOut
[in, out] Pointer to the XGVECTOR2 structure that is the result of the operation.
pV1
[in] Pointer to a source XGVECTOR2 structure, a position vector.
pT1
[in] Pointer to a source XGVECTOR2 structure, a tangent vector.
pV2
[in] Pointer to a source XGVECTOR2 structure, a position vector.
pT2
[in] Pointer to a source XGVECTOR2 structure, a tangent vector.
s
[in] Weighting factor. See Remarks.

Return Values

Pointer to a XGVECTOR2 structure that is the result of the Hermite spline interpolation.

Remarks

The XGVec2Hermite function interpolates from (positionA, tangentA) to (positionB, tangentB) using Hermite spline interpolation. This function interpolates between the position V1 and the tangent T1, when s is equal to 0, and between the position V2 and the tangent T2, when s is equal to 1.

Hermite splines are useful for controlling animation because the curve runs through all the control points. Also, because the position and tangent are explicitly specified at the ends of each segment, it is easy to create a C2 continuous curve as long as you make sure that your starting position and tangent match the ending values of the last segment.

The return value for this function is the same value returned in the pOut parameter. In this way, the XGVec2Hermite function can be used as a parameter for another function.

Requirements

  Header: Declared in xgmath.h.
  Import Library: Use xgraphics.lib.
